2606.09500.title: Deterministic Integrity Gates for LLM-Assisted Clinical Manuscript Preparation: An Auditable Biomedical Informatics Architecture
2606.09500.paper_summary: Objective. Large language models (LLMs) increasingly draft clinical research manuscripts, but their fluency can hide fabricated citations, numbers that drift from source tables, and unmet reporting-guideline items. Existing tools generate text without verifying it, and self-critique inherits the blind spots that produce confident fabrication. We describe an architecture that pairs generation with verification. Methods. The design rests on three principles: decompose the workflow into self-contained skills, gate every stage transition with halt-on-failure, and resolve each integrity question with the cheapest sufficient mechanism -- a deterministic, re-executable check where one suffices, and a prose-level probe only where interpretation is unavoidable. This determinism-where-possible split, organized as an integrity-gate taxonomy, is the core contribution. It is realized as MedSci Skills, an open-source toolkit of 43 skills coordinated by one orchestrator, whose deterministic tier comprises 21 standard-library detectors. We evaluate it on three reproducible public-dataset pipelines (STARD, PRISMA, STROBE) and a seeded-defect ablation. Results. Across the three pipelines every content-hash manifest verified clean and the gates surfaced real defects. On 27 identical injected defects the deterministic gates detected all 27 with no false positives on the matched clean fixtures, whereas a generic single-prompt LLM reviewer detected 11, its misses concentrated in generated-code, bibliography-internal, and style defects the prose does not expose. Conclusion. Determinism-where-possible verification yields an auditable, re-executable trail that exposes the evidence a human needs to check an LLM-assisted manuscript -- feasibility and reproducibility evidence, not a claim of human-competitive quality, which a separate blinded study addresses. MedSci Skills is MIT-licensed and archived (v3.8.0).
2606.09500.paper_summary_zh: 目標。大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地用於撰寫臨床研究手稿，但它們的流暢性可能掩蓋了虛構的引用、與來源表格偏離的數字以及未滿足的報告指導項目。現有工具生成文本而不進行驗證，自我批評繼承了產生自信虛構的盲點。我們描述了一種將生成與驗證相結合的架構。方法。該設計基於三個原則：將工作流程分解為自包含的技能，對每個階段的轉換進行故障時停止的門控，並用最便宜的充分機制解決每個完整性問題——在一個足夠的情況下進行確定性、可重執行的檢查，只有在無法避免解釋的情況下才進行散文級別的探查。這種在可能的情況下的確定性分割，組織為完整性門控分類法，是核心貢獻。它實現為 MedSci Skills，一個由一個協調者協調的 43 種技能的開源工具包，其確定性層包括 21 個標準庫檢測器。我們在三個可重複的公共數據集管道（STARD、PRISMA、STROBE）和一個種子缺陷消融上進行了評估。結果。在這三個管道中，每個內容哈希清單都驗證為乾淨，門控顯示了真實的缺陷。在 27 個相同的注入缺陷中，確定性門控檢測到了所有 27 個，並且在匹配的乾淨裝置上沒有假陽性，而一個通用的單提示 LLM 審稿人檢測到了 11 個，其漏檢集中在生成的代碼、文獻內部和散文未暴露的風格缺陷上。結論。在可能的情況下的確定性驗證產生了一條可審計、可重執行的痕跡，揭示了人類檢查 LLM 協助手稿所需的證據——可行性和可重複性證據，而不是人類競爭質量的聲明，這一點在一項單獨的盲測研究中有所探討。MedSci Skills 以 MIT 許可證授權並存檔（v3.8.0）。
2606.09500.author: Yoojin Nam et.al.
2606.09500.authors: Yoojin Nam, Jinhoon Jeong, Namkug Kim
